message.content:
Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate the Q6 launcher consolidation. Write /tmp/q6l_gate.md then 'cd /tmp && timeout 200 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/q6l_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -22' (embed inline; medium; no sandbox flags).
Evidence: build={"launcher_created":true,"variants_consolidated":9,"shims_written":9,"env_dicts_byte_identical":true,"skipped":[],"collection_after":"16738 tests collected, 11 errors (pre-existing, unrelated to launcher: mesh/auth/hle/clarification/perspective/playwright collection failures)"}, verify={"collection_ok":true,"oracle_matches":true,"oracle_sha":"9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98","variants_verified_identical":9,"dynamic_refs_intact":true} (env dicts byte-identical, oracle byte-identical 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98, dynamic refs intact).
Ask codex: "Is the run_full_scale consolidation behaviour-preserving (each old script still runs identically via a call-through shim, env profiles byte-identical, dynamic/by-path refs intact, no deleted-in-use file)? End with LAUNCHER-SAFE or LAUNCHER-REVISE."
Return verdict + points.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll write the gate file and run CODEX to evaluate the Q6 launcher consolidation.

input.content:
You are a strict release gate reviewer. Evaluate a refactor for behaviour-preservation ONLY (no aesthetics, no speculative scope).

## Context
A repo had 9 near-duplicate `run_full_scale*` launcher scripts (Q6 launcher family). They were consolidated into a single parameterized launcher. Each of the 9 old script paths was replaced by a thin call-through shim that re-invokes the new launcher with the correct variant profile, so any caller (human, cron, CI, dynamic/by-path reference) that targets an old path still works.

## Evidence (machine-produced)
build = {
  "launcher_created": true,
  "variants_consolidated": 9,
  "shims_written": 9,
  "env_dicts_byte_identical": true,
  "skipped": [],
  "collection_after": "16738 tests collected, 11 errors (pre-existing, unrelated to launcher: mesh/auth/hle/clarification/perspective/playwright collection failures)"
}
verify = {
  "collection_ok": true,
  "oracle_matches": true,
  "oracle_sha": "9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98",
  "variants_verified_identical": 9,
  "dynamic_refs_intact": true
}

Key invariants asserted by the tooling:
- All 9 variants' env/profile dicts are byte-identical between old scripts and the new launcher's per-variant profiles.
- The behaviour oracle is byte-identical (sha 9c0a3d43...): the launcher's resolved config/command for every variant matches the pre-refactor baseline.
- Dynamic / by-path references are intact (nothing that resolves an old script path at runtime is broken).
- No old script file was deleted while still in use — each remains as a call-through shim.
- Test collection after the change: 16738 collected with 11 pre-existing errors unrelated to the launcher.

## Your task
Assess: Is the run_full_scale consolidation behaviour-preserving? Specifically:
1. Each old script still runs identically via a call-through shim.
2. Env profiles byte-identical.
3. Dynamic/by-path refs intact.
4. No deleted-in-use file.

Reason about residual risks the evidence does NOT cover (e.g. shim argv/exit-code/stdin passthrough, working-directory assumptions, `__file__`/`$0`-relative path resolution inside the launcher, env inheritance vs full replacement, signal handling). State whether the evidence is sufficient to call it safe, and name any gap that would flip the verdict.

End your answer with exactly one token on its own line: LAUNCHER-SAFE or LAUNCHER-REVISE.

content.content: File created successfully at: /tmp/q6l_gate.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

input.description: Run CODEX gate on Q6 launcher consolidation

content.content:
The evidence is not sufficient for a strict behaviour-preservation approval.

- Old paths exist and map to the nine variants, but end-to-end shim equivalence is not demonstrated.
- Env/profile dictionaries are byte-identical.
- Dynamic/by-path references are verified intact.
- No in-use legacy path was deleted.
- The oracle proves resolved configuration and command equivalence, not process-level invocation semantics.
- Test collection does not exercise launcher runtime behaviour.

The verdict would flip if all nine legacy paths were invoked end-to-end and shown to preserve:

- Exact argv, including quoting, empty arguments, and `--`.
- Exit codes and stdin/stdout/stderr/TTY behavior.
- Signal propagation, ideally through `exec`-style call-through.
- Invocation from arbitrary working directories.
- Correct launcher resolution without unsafe `$0`/`__file__` assumptions.
- Environment inheritance, overrides, and unsetting semantics.
- Executable permissions and shebang behavior.

Any failure in those areas is externally observable and would make the consolidation non-preserving.

LAUNCHER-REVISE

content.text: CODEX returned LAUNCHER-REVISE. The gate flagged that the evidence proves config/command-resolution equivalence (the oracle) but not process-level invocation semantics of the shims.

codex_points: Evidence is insufficient for strict behaviour-preservation approval: the oracle proves resolved config/command equivalence, NOT process-level invocation semantics of the shims.
What IS verified and accepted: env/profile dicts byte-identical, dynamic/by-path references intact, no in-use legacy path deleted, oracle byte-identical (sha 9c0a3d43...) across all 9 variants.
Core gap: old paths map to the 9 variants but end-to-end shim equivalence is never demonstrated - no legacy path was actually invoked end-to-end.
Test collection (16738 collected, 11 pre-existing unrelated errors) does not exercise launcher runtime behaviour, so it adds no runtime assurance.
To flip to SAFE, invoke all 9 legacy paths end-to-end and show preservation of: exact argv (quoting, empty args, `--`), exit codes, stdin/stdout/stderr/TTY behaviour.
Also must preserve: signal propagation (ideally via exec-style call-through), invocation from arbitrary working directories, launcher resolution without unsafe `$0`/`__file__` assumptions.
Also must preserve: environment inheritance/override/unset semantics, executable permissions, and shebang behaviour.
Any failure in those areas is externally observable and would make the consolidation non-preserving - hence the strict REVISE despite clean oracle/env evidence.
